title: cerastium ledebourianum
intro: Cerastium ledebourianum, also known as the snow-in-summer or Ledebour's whitlow grass, is a low-growing, perennial plant belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cerastium ledebourianum include its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reduce inflammation and pain, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be applied topically or taken internally to support overall health and well-being.
